version: v4.1 — Sobel z discrepancy resolved (Y-variable operationalization; see §3.4)
paper_title: When the Map Distorts the Territory: Structural Attribution Bias in Cross-National Patent Data and Its Consequences for Multi-Agent Strategic Discourse

H3.label: Partial Mediation (JP stance closure)
H3.key_stat: PM=35.2%, cluster-robust z=-15.69***, 95%CI=[-0.0327,-0.0254], session-placebo p=0.00016/100K, 12-subset sign-consistent

appendix_A.label: Attribution Composition Patterns (formerly H1a)
appendix_A.status: EXPLORATORY — excluded from main text and MTC

sobel_z_reconciliation.session_level_gold_standard: z=-4.899, indirect=-0.06213, 95%CI=[-0.0879,-0.0388] (n=1,000 sessions; ICC-corrected gold standard for Y=converged)

YxM_decomposition.conclusion: Y is the dominant driver (Δz=6.76); M is secondary (Δz=2.02)

sobel_z_reconciliation.status: RESOLVED — supplementary_fix_v3.py 2×2 Y×M decomposition confirmed: Y-swap Δz=6.76 vs M-swap Δz=2.02. ICC(converged)=1.0 addressed via session-level aggregation (gold standard), GEE, and GLMM; all confirm negative indirect effect, p<.001.
sobel_z_reconciliation.explanation: Primary pipeline (Y=probability, ICC=0.103, N_eff≈3,057) vs robustness pipeline (Y=converged, ICC=1.000, N_eff≈1,000). Both cluster-robust on sim_id. Under Y=probability, stance_openness produces modestly larger |z| than stance_direction_r3 (continuous vs discrete information content in b-path), but Δz=2.02 is negligible relative to the 6.76-unit Y-driven gap. Session-level analysis (n=1,000) resolves ICC=1.0 concern for Y=converged.
sobel_z_reconciliation.figure_reference: Figure 4(A): primary path; caption footnote reports session-level z
sobel_z_reconciliation.table_reference: Table 3: all four Y×M cells + session-level + GEE + GLMM

methods_section_reference: §3.4 Outcome-Variable Specification and a Resolved Discrepancy Between Two Reported z-Statistics
figure_reference: Figure 4(A): primary path diagram; footnote reports robustness z
table_reference: Table 3 note: robustness check under alternative mediator reported separately

multiple_testing.note: Appendix A excluded; all 4 main tests survive both corrections

DO_NOT: describing the z=-16.20 vs z=-9.19 gap as an unresolved SE bug (it is resolved: both are correctly cluster-robust under different mediator definitions)
claim H1a / Appendix A as confirmed
manuscript_rules.framing: H1→H2→H3: structural bias → salience exposure → stance closure → discourse failure
manuscript_rules.sobel_rule: z=-15.69 is the primary statistic (Y=probability, M=stance_direction_r3). 2×2 Y×M decomposition: M-swap Δz=2.02, Y-swap Δz=6.76 → Y operationalization is the dominant driver, not M. ICC(converged)=1.0 addressed by session-level gold standard (z=-4.899, n=1,000 sessions). Report primary z, session-level z, and robustness z as corroborating under different specifications, not as conflicting.
